text: International Friendship Day 2026! | Singapore: Forging Friendships in a Complex World

What is IFD? | International Friendship Day (IFD for short) is a day dedicated to celebrating different cultures of various nationalities. | It encourages people of different nationalities to come together and unite, looking past our differences. | It is actually commemorated on April 10 of 2026

What is our theme for IFD? | The theme is “ | Singapore: Forging friendships in a complex world.” | A complex world where so many different things are happening, yet we are all able to come together. | Even with all our differences and diversity, we can make friendships with one another.
Our  | ASEAN  | countries | Singapore | Philippines | Vietnam | Malaysia | Indonesia | Myanmar | Laos | Brunei | Thailand | Cambodia | Timor-Leste | (Joined 26 Oct 2025)

Cam | bo | dia | A holiday celebrated in Cambodia: | Khmer New Year (Chaul Chnam Thmey): In April, this is the premier time for strengthening bonds. It is a time for friends and family to gather, exchange gifts, play traditional games, and visit pagodas together. | (1) Traditional dish: | Fish Amok | : A creamy, coconut curry steamed in banana leaves, featuring lemongrass, turmeric, and lime leaves.

Viet | nam |  A holiday celebrated in Vietnam: | Tết |  (Lunar New Year) : |   Friends are honoured alongside family, with the second day of Tết often dedicated to visiting, exchanging greetings, and strengthening bonds through shared traditional activities and gatherings. | (1) Traditonal dish: | Phở |  (Noodle Soup): The national dish, typically beef or chicken broth, rice noodles, and fresh herbs.

L | a | o | s | A holiday celebrated in Laos: | Baci Ceremony (Soun Baci): This is a fundamental Lao ritual performed to celebrate important life events, welcome guests, and bring good luck and prosperity to friends and family. Elders or knowledgeable individuals tie white cotton strings around a person's wrist while offering blessings for health and happiness. | (1) Traditional dish: | Larb (Laap) | : The national dish, a spicy minced meat salad (chicken, pork, or duck) mixed with lime, chili, herbs, and toasted rice powder.
